title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox's top four batters managed just one hit against Dodgers pitcher Dustin May.
Suggestions include moving Wilyer Abreu to the cleanup spot and Masataka Yoshida to leadoff.
Potential call-up candidates from Worcester include Mickey Gasper, batting .400 with three home runs.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs with a 144-118 victory over the Pelicans.
Boston tied the NBA's single-game three-point record by making 29 three-pointers for the second time in history.
Jaylen Brown scored 23 points while managing Achilles tendinitis; Jayson Tatum was rested ahead of playoffs.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Pizza Movie" featuring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone is streaming on Hulu, offering a comedic drug-fueled quest.
"Sirat," an Oscar-nominated film set in Morocco amidst geopolitical tension, is available on Hulu.
"The Testaments," a sequel to "The Handmaid's Tale," explores dystopian Boston on Hulu with Ann Dowd reprising her role.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will not play against the Pelicans due to right Achilles repair management.
Jaylen Brown is available despite left Achilles tendinitis; he missed the previous game for the Celtics.
Three Boston starters, Hauser, Queta, and White, are probable but will play against New Orleans.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her daughter Emma's cancer battle.
Emma was diagnosed with a medulloblastoma mass in April 2025, requiring immediate surgery and extensive treatment.
The family has endured numerous medical challenges, including relocation for Emma's proton radiation treatment in Philadelphia.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not debut for the Bruins against Tampa Bay on Saturday, as confirmed by coach Marco Sturm.
Sturm emphasizes patience with 19-year-old prospect Hagens, citing his need to adjust to the team's system.
Bruins aim to clinch a playoff spot with a win, but Hagens may not play in the final three regular-season games.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers featured on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list.
Forbes' historic list includes Massachusetts figures like Benjamin Franklin and Sheldon Adelson, highlighting their self-made rises.
Forbes evaluates candidates based on 'Self-Made Scores' including financial success, challenges overcome, and long-term impact.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon takes place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year's marathon includes 30,000 runners from over 130 countries, with six start waves introduced for the first time.
Live coverage of the marathon will be available on WCVB Channel 5 and through the Very Local app.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are hiring Shane Bowen, former Giants and Titans defensive coordinator, as an analyst.
Bowen previously worked with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at the Titans and on Ohio State's staff.
Patriots defensive coordinator Zak Kuhr considers Bowen a mentor, indicating a strong working relationship.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston provides public restrooms in libraries, community centers, and unexpected places like firehouses and some restaurants.
The city's Age Strong Commission has developed an interactive map locating public restrooms across Boston neighborhoods.
Under Massachusetts law, people with certain medical conditions can access employee-only restrooms if public facilities aren't nearby.
